avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The existing snowpack will remain well bonded and stable. Isolated, shallow deposits of fresh moderately bonded windslab will develop in some wind sheltered areas through the afternoon. Coire rims and gully tops with a North-East to South-East aspect over 1000 metres will be most affected. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Heavy rain at all levels overnight. Precipitation will turn to snow on the summits from late morning gradually turning to snow at lower levels as precipitation eases. The freezing level will fall to around 800 metres by the end of the afternoon. Winds will initially be very Strong Southerly becoming moderate North-Westerly by the end of the forecast period.
Observed Weather Influences: A mild wet day. The freezing level was above the summits with strong mainly Southerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is well bonded and stable. A very gradual thaw of the remaining snow continues. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Isolated accumulations of windslab will develop during the afternoon above 1000 metres on North-East to South-East aspects, these will be shallow and generally avoidable and confined to gully tops and coire rims. Elsewhere the snowpack will remain well bonded and stable on all aspects and elevations. The greatest snow cover will remain in high coires and plateau areas above 800 metres, with snow patches and ribbons extending to lower elevations. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Gale force Southerly winds will steadily ease through the day to become West to North-Westerlies. Spells of light rain is expected at all levels, this will turn to snow during the afternoon as the freezing level falls from above the summits to 1000 metres by the end of the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Gale force Southerly winds with spells of light rain at all levels. The freezing level was above the summits.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack surface is thawing and is generally stable on all aspects and elevations. The greatest snow cover remains in high coires and plateau areas above 800 metres, with snow patches and ribbons extending to lower elevations.  The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snow will be well bonded and stable throughout. Greatest cover will be found above 900 metres on South-West through West to North aspects and on high plateau areas.  The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: A dry night, with drizzle arriving by morning. Light rain during the afternoon will turn to sleet or wet snow on the highest summits by the end of the period. Strong Southerly winds overnight will ease to fresh during the day as they veer to the North-West by late afternoon. Freezing level will be over the summits, eventually lowering to 1100 metres towards the end of the period.
Observed Weather Influences: Drizzle or light rain in low cloud. Strong South-South-Westerly winds with some powerful gusts. Freezing level gently rising to above the summits.
ObservedSnowStability: The snow is well bonded and stable throughout. Surface layers are starting to soften but very firm near-surface layers are present. Best cover is to be found above 900 metres on the high plateau areas and on South-West through West to North aspects. Very patchy cover below this altitude. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Overnight the snowpack will be thawing at all elevations and any remaining cornices will be weak and prone to collapse. Although fresh snow amounts are not expected to be significant, there is potential for isolated deposits of windslab developing as the freezing level drops during the afternoon. These will be confined to wind-sheltered locations on North-East to South-East aspects above 950 metres. Elsewhere the snowpack will become firm and be stable. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Rain overnight with the freezing level above the summits. Rain will gradually turn to snow throughout the afternoon as the freezing level drops to around 800 metres. Gale force Southerly winds will ease through the day to become moderate while veering West-North-West.
Observed Weather Influences: Damp and poor visibility with the freezing level above the summits. Strong Southerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack has good stability in all locations and was firm initially. The snow surface started to soften throughout the daytime. Snow cover is now mostly confined to North-West through North to Easterly aspects above 850 metres, with only patches remaining below this level, which are continuing to thaw. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: New snow amounts are not expected to be extensive however, shallow windslab may develop in wind sheltered locations. Deepest deposits can be expected in the afternoon on N to E aspects particularly around the tops of gullies and steep coire rims above 850m. with soft cornices developing. Elsewhere the snowpack will be generally well bonded and stable. Largest snow areas will be on NW, through N to E aspects above 870m, snow cover will be more patchy elsewhere. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Overnight. the freezing level will be well above the summits it will start to lower around mid morning steadily falling ending at around 800 metres by late afternoon. Rain showers overnight becoming more persistent in the morning turning to light snow showers mostly in the afternoon. The strong-gale South-South-Easterly winds will ease veering South easing further becoming Westerly.
Observed Weather Influences: Today, it was misty above 600 metres with poor visibility, strong South-Easterly winds with light rain at all levels. Summit temperatures were plus 2.2 degrees Celsius.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is well bonded, very firm and stable. Largest continuous snow areas remain on North-West through North to East aspects above around 870 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The patchy and limited snowpack that remains mostly above 750 metres on North-West through North to Easterly aspects, although moist and diminishing in extent, will remain stable throughout. This will become firmer  later in the afternoon as it refreezes. New snow accumulations above 600m are not expected to be significant. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Mild temperatures will continue overnight and during the day, freezing level overnight at 1400 metres will lower during the Thursday afternoon to 800 metres or so. Precipitation falling as rain at all levels overnight will gradually turn to sleet and snow above 600m as it cools in the afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: A mild day with a freezing level well above the summits, temperatures plus 2 deg C on the tops.
Winds strong Southerly. Showers of rain at all levels. Poor visibility.
ObservedSnowStability: The patchy and limited snowpack that remains mostly above 750 metres on North-West through North to Easterly aspects continues to diminish in the mild conditions. Although moist it remains well bonded and stable throughout. The avalanche hazard is Low.
